subcategory: OTP User Enumeration and Brute Force

steps.vulnerability_name: Fix validateOtp — stop leaking user email and return 400 for bad OTP
steps.description: Replace EntityNotFoundException throw (which leaks the submitted email address in the error body) with a generic 400 response. Also change the invalid-OTP status from 500 to 400 so the controller returns the correct HTTP status code.

steps.vulnerability_name: Fix secureValidateOtp — stop leaking user email and fix 500 status codes
steps.description: Replace EntityNotFoundException throw with a generic 400 response in secureValidateOtp. Also change invalid-OTP and error status codes from 500 to 400.

steps.vulnerability_name: Fix checkOtp controller — return HTTP 400 instead of 500 for bad OTP
steps.description: The /v2/check-otp handler maps all non-200 service responses to HTTP 500. Change it to return HTTP 400 (Bad Request) so the OTP failure path has the correct status code.

steps.vulnerability_name: Fix secureCheckOtp controller — return HTTP 400 instead of 500 for bad OTP
steps.description: The /v3/check-otp handler maps all non-200/503 service responses to HTTP 500. Change the fallback to HTTP 400 so invalid-OTP and user-not-found paths return the correct status code.

steps.vulnerability_name: Add Bucket4j rate-limiting dependency to build.gradle.kts
steps.description: Add the Bucket4j core library so the OtpRateLimitFilter can use in-memory token-bucket rate limiting with no external infrastructure.

steps.vulnerability_name: Create OtpRateLimitFilter — IP-based rate limiter for OTP endpoints
steps.description: New Servlet filter that allows 5 OTP attempts per IP per 10 minutes across /check-otp. Excess requests receive HTTP 429 with a JSON body. File must be created at the path below.

steps.vulnerability_name: Register OtpRateLimitFilter in WebSecurityConfig and add to filter chain
steps.description: Autowire the new filter and add it to the Spring Security filter chain so it runs before the JWT authentication filter.

steps.vulnerability_name: Add OtpRateLimitFilter to the security filter chain
steps.description: Insert the rate-limit filter before the JWT token filter so it intercepts OTP requests first.

verification.description: Unknown user OTP — must return 400 and must not echo the submitted email in the body
Wrong OTP on v3 — must return 400, not 500
Rate limit — 6th rapid OTP attempt must return 429
verification.curl: for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w 'attempt '$i': %{http_code}\n' -X POST http://localhost:8888/identity/api/auth/v3/check-otp -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"otp":"1234","password":"x","email":"test@test.com"}'; done
